responses:
Hi, My name is Lexi, I'm here to make you more productive. 
Some shortcuts for you: 
	/help - Open the list of commands
Have a nice day
Bye! Remember to look for me if you need more help
Happy to help!
Any time!
My pleasure

Here are your options: 
	/learn - learn how to make yourself more productive from famous authors 
	/bookrec - Book recommendations for improved productivity

One important takeaway from the book Atomic Habits is to have a well constructed system to work towards your goal. Because no matter how difficult it is to get to your goal, you won't be able to accomplish it without a functioning system. 
 An example will be to create small milestones for yourself to complete, slowly working towards your final goal.

From the book Thinking Fast and Slow: 
	 When you are sad, you are more likely to be incapable of performing intuitive tasks completely. When you are happy, you lose control of thinking logically, and instead perform better at intuitive tasks.

Something to learn from the book Getting Things Done: 
	 A critical step for getting things done is to define the following task in order to make rapid progress.

Here's a book that you should try out!: 
	Atomic Habits - James Clear

Check out this book!: 
	The 80/20 Principle - Richard Koch

Try this out!: 
	Thinking, Fast and Slow - Daniel Kahneman

You might be interested in this: 
	Dopamine Nation - Anna Lembke

Give this a look: 
	The Power of Habit - Charles Duhigg

This is worth seeing: 
	Make Time - Jake Knapp

Pay attention to this: 
	Building a Second Brain - Tiago Forte

Have a look at this: 
	The Checklist Manifesto - Atul Gawande

Look what I found: 
	The Bullet Journal Method - Ryder Carroll

Can't miss this!: 
	Hyperfocus - Chris Bailey

What did the horse say after it tripped? Help! 
	I’ve fallen and I can’t giddyup!

Why can’t you hear a pterodactyl going to the bathroom? 
	Because the “P” is silent.

What do you call a well-balanced horse? 
	Stable

What do you call an angry carrot? 
	A steamed veggie

Where do polar bears keep their money? 
	In a snowbank

How do you make an egg-roll? 
	You push it!

What would bears be without bees? 
	Ears.

What do you call a pile of cats? 
	A meow-tain

Why do cows wear bells? 
	Because their horns don’t work.

Why did the bicycle fall over? 
	Because it was two tired.
meow?
meow
purr
hiss, just kidding!
growl, hungry :/
chirrup
yowl
trill
chatter

chirp
squeak

grumble, food?
snarl

grunt, where is my free cookie?
Sometimes being nice and controlling your emotions will make you more productive.
